---
title: "Zigzag Bladderwort (Utricularia subulata)"
slug: "utricularia-subulata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622983424/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Zigzag Bladderwort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Lentibulariaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Lentibulariaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622983424/medium.jpg" alt="Zigzag Bladderwort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jmull28">
jmull28</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342135893">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Lentibulariaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=79468)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A tiny carnivorous plant characterized by its thread-like stems and small bladder traps, along with slender flower stalks that often have a slightly zigzag appearance. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist, sandy habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and seepage areas. It typically blooms during the warmer months, producing small yellow flowers, and obtains nutrients by trapping microscopic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/79468-Utricularia-subulata)
-   [Local profile](https://www.wildflower.org/plants/result.php?id_plant=UTSU)
